arch_source: RTX 5090 (cap 12.0) live torch.cuda.get_device_properties + CUDA C Programming Guide CC 12.0; cached tile-prover/hw-profiles/sm120.json
decomposition: ROUND 2: full PRE mapping fused. _hc_premap_fwd/bwd_kernel fuses rms(over n*C) + softmax(dim=-1) + softmax(dim=-2) + Cayley(3-iter, unrolled 4x4 scalar matmuls) + colmean/rowsum reductions + x_bar contraction into ONE register-resident kernel (fp32 internal, one program=one token, n=4/iters=3 unrolled). The proj GEMV is kept as a single cuBLAS addmm in the CARRIER dtype (bf16 -> tensor-core, fp32 accumulate) because folding it per-token would reload proj_w[48,3072] per program and lose tensor cores; backward grad_w/grad_h_proj also bf16/tensor-core GEMMs. Analytic backward (softmax both-dim jacobians + 3-step Cayley reverse recurrence + skew + /rms + proj VJP) validated vs autograd at rel ~1e-7 (ignore/derive_pre_backward.py) before coding. ROUND 1 (still in place): POST x_mix+x_post+add fused; PRE x_bar kept as a standalone kernel for the sinkhorn/eager fallback. Sinkhorn + CPU + iters!=3 + n!=4 fall back to hc_pre_map_reference (eager).

bank_conflict_free.method: Z3 (vacuous: no explicit shared memory allocated; all 6 kernels register-resident, mapping done in fp32 scalar regs)

in_bounds.method: Z3 UNSAT on negation, all tensors (h/xbar/hpre/hpost/hres + round-2 raw48/rms) x {B4S4096, B2S512}

global_coalesced.method: Z3: stride-1 contiguous + single 128B line + uniform scalar broadcast (Hres/Hpost/Hpre + round-2 raw_full/rms)

tile_validity_sm120.conditional_on: register count is set by ptxas at compile time; estimate (incl. round-2 mapping scalars) is conservative and may spill to local rather than exceed 255/thread. Empirically all configs launched on the live 5090 with zero resource failures.

gates.profiler_note: profiler uses a tiny Linear(C,C) sublayer so HC fraction is an UPPER bound; remaining copy_/mul are mostly cuBLAS GEMM transposes + POST grad_y + the profiler's own loss casts, not the mapping storm (which is gone: bmm 0, mul 800->100).
